recentPosts.title:
The most important meeting at Atlys lasts 3 minutes. No one needs to show up.

Every day at exactly 3:01 PM, a customer's phone call plays across our office.

Just a short recording from a real traveler who called our support team.

Then everyone goes back to work.

We call it the 3:01 Call 📞

As companies grow, it's easy to drift from the people you're building for. Not because anyone intends it, just because the days fill up. Tickets, dashboards, sprint planning, OKRs, internal discussions.

The customer slowly becomes a metric.

The 3:01 Call is a reminder that behind every application is a real person. Someone figuring out an upcoming trip, a family event, a honeymoon, a work opportunity, or simply the uncertainty of whether they'll make it on time.

No fancy setup. No forced attendance. If you're in the office, you hear it. That's enough.

Over the next few weeks, I'll share a few small but meaningful culture rituals we've built at Atlys, just simple things that help us stay connected to what matters as we grow 🙂

projects.name: BALAKALAKAAR 2015
projects.description: Balakalakaar is an annual initiative taken up by the local office of AIESEC in Delhi IIT. It is organized with the aim of creating an opportunity for the under privileged children of the city to discover and develop their potential in creative exercises such as Art, Dance, Drama, Music, and Craft. This is done primarily through workshops and training provided by esteemed professionals in the above fields along with AIESECers, youth volunteers and internationals from the Global Community… Show more Balakalakaar is an annual initiative taken up by the local office of AIESEC in Delhi IIT. It is organized with the aim of creating an opportunity for the under privileged children of the city to discover and develop their potential in creative exercises such as Art, Dance, Drama, Music, and Craft. This is done primarily through workshops and training provided by esteemed professionals in the above fields along with AIESECers, youth volunteers and internationals from the Global Community Development Program.Event ApproachNumber of NGO to be Approached: 52Number of Children to be Impacted: 2500+Referred links:http://www.fuccha.in/volunteer-at-balakalakaar-2015-aiesec-in-delhi-iit Show less
